TRS - Schizophrenia - Negative symtoms + look for need for clozapine +SSRI + other interventions done - ADLS and supports + ACT +Behavioural activation +ECT 

- Basically plan with patient opinion was excluded , not a person centric approach, likes and dislikes were not included - strength and deficit based approach 
- Assess for NDIA outcome and need for early modification to avoid resource wasting and include the patient opinion +OT and Psychosis opinion and family 

- Psychoeducation about the educations and gradual integration program - consideration with TAFE one to one support tutoring, initially and gradually build the in person and attending the college 

- focus is always on recovery and relapse prevention is part of recovery. Therefore focus should be sym recovery, functional recovery +community integration +max utilisation of the resource and cost effective way - education to the family and include the NDIS coordinator and OT_Psychologist for replanning and offer educational supports

AOD - service - dual diagnsis role 
28 yo women - vulnerable, personality + comorbid anxiety + depressions +psychosis + relapse in mind 
currently on maintenance phase + pregnancy + UDS regular checks should be in mind + risk of relapse 
reassure patient about the pregnancy - positive  for pregnancy does not trigger CPF + Must meet the criteria + however explain about the monitoring + safety of methadone in pregnancy + lactation issues to change to methylphenidate because methadone impact the lactation+ 
no supportive partner + fdv screening + pathways to include partner to treatment + social worker involvement + enhance family/ NOK/ friends supports 
existing CPF + high risk and duty of care to inform about current pregnancy might change the coping and risk of child+ 
support with SSRi and therapy, detox benzodiazepines + foetal monitoring + perinatal involvement + MDT trigger with GP
